title: FRQ AI grading paused — write FRQs and grade externally
body: Due to high demand and limited grading resources, our AI grader is temporarily paused. You can still write any FRQ — when you submit, you'll see your full response next to the official rubric, plus a one-click button to copy the question, rubric, and your response in one block so you can paste it into your own AI tool (Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude) for feedback. We'll bring AI grading back as soon as we can.

title: Open the original PDF if an FRQ stimulus looks off
body: If anything in an FRQ — a passage, chart, or image — looks like a typo, formatting bug, or is hard to read, tap "View original PDF" near the question to open the College Board source and see exactly how it was printed on the real exam.

title: Adi stays available — removed shared daily ceiling
body: Adi was occasionally going dark for everyone once a shared daily safety cap was hit, even if you personally had messages left. That shared ceiling is gone. Your own per-day Adi allowance is unchanged, and Adi should now stay reachable throughout the day.

title: Fairer essay grading from Adi
body: Three fixes to FRQ grading on essays and long responses: (1) Adi now has clear anchors for what earns thesis, contextualization, evidence, reasoning, and complexity rows on LEQs, DBQs, and argument essays — so a real attempt with named historical examples and a counterargument earns the points instead of getting denied for vague reasons. (2) When Adi's written feedback is clearly positive but the score is 0, the score is now corrected upward to match what the feedback says. (3) Adi can no longer deny points using fallback phrases like "lacks clarity" or "vague" on substantive answers — a concrete factual or scope-related reason is required.

title: FRQ grader stops denying points for synonym mismatches
body: If your answer makes the comparison or connection the rubric asks for — even using different words than the example — Adi will now credit it. Specific cases: citing both parties' percentages on data prompts, naming both cases' facts on SCOTUS comparisons, and using synonyms like "coalitions of citizens" instead of "organized groups."

title: More consistent FRQ grading from Adi
body: Three fixes to how Adi grades FRQs: (1) named leaders, cases, and documents are now graded with the same bar regardless of how canonical the name is, so a less common but defensible answer earns the same as a textbook one, (2) for math FRQs, when your final numerical answer matches the rubric, you get the answer point even if Adi was uncertain, and (3) Adi will no longer say "no response submitted" on long answers.

title: Fixed Adi showing "taking a break" when it shouldn't have
body: Adi was incorrectly telling many of you it had hit a daily limit, even when you'd barely used it. The internal safety cap is now calibrated correctly — Adi should stay available throughout the day.

title: Send us feedback or report a bug
body: There's a new Feedback link in the top bar — use it to tell us about bugs, request features, flag content issues, or send any other thoughts. We read every one.

title: AP Comparative Government is here
body: Practice questions, drills, and study guides are now live for all 5 units of AP Comparative Government — covering the six core countries (China, Iran, Mexico, Nigeria, Russia, UK). Pick AP Comp Gov from the home screen to get started.

title: Fixed Adi crashing mid-chat
body: Adi was failing on a lot of messages because of an issue with our AI provider. Switched to a more stable setup — Adi should respond reliably again.

title: FRQ grading credits creative-but-valid answers
body: If your response to an FRQ is a defensible answer that doesn't exactly match the rubric's listed examples, Adi now gives you the point. Also fixed a bug where Adi would zero out a point even when its own feedback said you got it right — that no longer happens on paraphrased evidence.

title: FRQ grading is faster and more consistent
body: Adi grades FRQs faster now and with cleaner scoring logic — your scores should feel fairer. Quick or accidental submissions like 'idk' or 'asdf' no longer count against your daily grading limit.

title: Adi and FRQ now have separate daily limits
body: You get 30 Adi messages and 3 FRQ grades per day. Burning through FRQs no longer locks you out of Adi (and vice versa). Limits reset overnight.

title: FRQ grader actually works now
body: Fixed some bugs in the FRQ grader that were giving inaccurate scores. Grading is now more accurate — re-grade any essays that felt off.

title: AP Gov Unit 3 vocab list refreshed
body: The Civil Liberties and Civil Rights study guide now matches what your teachers actually highlight — every bolded vocab term plus the AP-required Supreme Court cases (Engel, Tinker, Brown, etc.) and foundational documents (Bill of Rights, Letter from a Birmingham Jail).

title: We'll show what's new each visit
body: When something changes on the site, you'll see a quick popup the first time you come back. Just X it out to keep practicing.

title: Drill answers accept common abbreviations
body: Federalist papers now accept 'fed 10' or 'federalist 10'. Parenthetical aliases like 'elastic clause' work for 'Necessary and Proper Clause'. World War I and World War 1 are treated the same.
